title: myriophyllum pedunculatum subsp. longibracteolatum
intro: Myriophyllum pedunculatum subsp. longibracteolatum, also known as the Australian water-milfoil, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the water-milfoil family (Haloragaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Australian and Asian cultures, for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Australian water-milfoil can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its herbal benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
